name: glycoengineering
tagline: Scans protein sequences for N/O-glycosylation sites and links out to external glycoengineering tools and databases.
verdict: Clean, on-topic bioinformatics skill: plain Python regex/heuristic code, no obfuscation or hidden instructions. Minor flags: it queries public third-party APIs (GlyConnect, DTU) with sequence/UniProt data and tells the user to `pip install glycoshield` from an unpinned source, both disclosed but unconfirmed before execution.

highlights.summary: query_glyconnect() sends a UniProt ID to glyconnect.expasy.org via requests.get; disclosed in the skill's stated purpose ('access curated glycoengineering tools') but fires with no explicit user confi

Instructs `pip install glycoshield` (line 232) from PyPI with no version pin or checksum, and points to a GitLab-hosted research tool (line 226) — standard for a scientific skill but technically an un
No explicit 'do NOT use when' guidance (e.g., non-protein sequences, DNA/RNA input, or when experimental validation is required) is stated anywhere.
